failure_surface: TypeError raised during wb.save(): 'expected <class 'openpyxl.styles.fills.Fill'' indicating invalid assignment to the fill property.
root_cause: Agent attempted to assign None to a Cell's fill property (cell_q.fill = None). In openpyxl, the fill property requires a Fill object instance; None is not an acceptable value. Clearing format requires using a valid PatternFill instance (e.g., PatternFill()) or avoiding the assignment entirely.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before modifying any style properties (fill, font, border, etc.), verify the target type accepts the assigned value. When attempting to clear/reset a style in openpyxl, use an empty style object (e.g., PatternFill(fill_type='none')) instead of None.
evidence: Error traceback shows TypeError at wb.save() line 84 with message 'expected <class 'openpyxl.styles.fills.Fill'>'
Code contains 'cell_q.fill = None' in the else branch designed to clear fills for non-matching rows
openpyxl library was used for all spreadsheet manipulation throughout the trajectory
Other fill assignments in the same codeblock correctly used PatternFill objects (yellow_fill, green_fill)
No prior verification of what values are acceptable for the fill property before assignment

memory_items.title: Style Property Type Safety
memory_items.lesson: When setting style properties in openpyxl, always assign valid style object instances (Fill, Font, Border, Alignment), never None or primitive types.
memory_items.generalization: Verify each style property's expected type before assignment; clearing a style requires an empty/descriptive style object, not None.

memory_items.title: Clear Formatting in openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: To reset/clear cell formatting in openpyxl, create a PatternFill with fill_type='none' rather than assigning None to the fill attribute.
memory_items.generalization: PatternFill(patternType=None) or PatternFill(start_color='FFFFFF', end_color='FFFFFF', fill_type='none') effectively clears fill without violating type constraints.

memory_items.title: Pre-Save Validation for Style Objects
memory_items.lesson: Add validation checks before workbook.save() when extensive style modifications are performed, ensuring all style properties reference valid objects.
memory_items.generalization: Invalid style assignments may not raise errors immediately but will cause exceptions during serialization (save); validate style objects in conditional branches that modify formatting.

edits.content: - **Style Assignment**: All style properties (fill, font, border, alignment) require valid style object instances from openpyxl.styles. Never assign None to these properties; to clear formatting, use patternType='none' or equivalent empty style objects (e.g., `PatternFill(patternType=None)`).
